message:
fix(ci): Use python3 instead of python for CPython reference tests

The CI was building Python 3.13.1 successfully but test commands
were using 'python' which resolved to system Python 3.12, causing
all tests to fail with ModuleNotFoundError.

Root cause:
- Verification step: python3 --version → correct (3.13.1)
- Test execution: python -m test → wrong (3.12 system default)

Fix:
- Change all test commands from 'python' to 'python3'
- Ensures PATH resolution picks up /tmp/python-install/bin/python3

Affected jobs:
- test-cpython-core1 (line 484)
- test-cpython-core2 (line 556)
- test-cpython-core3 (line 628)
- test-cpython-linux (line 700)
- test-cpython-macos (line 769)

Expected result: 94/95 tests passing (with test_datetime potentially fixed)

files.patch:
@@ -481,7 +481,7 @@ jobs:
             [ -z "$test" ] && continue
             TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             # Use Python's built-in test module
-            if python -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
+            if python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
               PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
             else
               FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
@@ -553,7 +553,7 @@ jobs:
             [ -z "$test" ] && continue
             TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             # Use Python's built-in test module
-            if python -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
+            if python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
               PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
             else
               FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
